habitat: Rocky meadows and open woodland of cold Central Asia
blurb: Pigsqueak: bold, glossy, cabbage-like evergreen leaves that turn burnished bronze-red through winter, topped by stout clusters of pink flowers in earliest spring. Iron-tough and one of the very few bold evergreen leaves for dry shade.
size: 12–18 in tall in bloom × 18–24 in wide
sun: Part shade to full shade (tolerates sun with moisture)
water: Low to moderate; drought-tolerant once established
spread: Spreads slowly by thick rhizomes into evergreen clumps; a good edger and groundcover
seasons: Big leathery evergreen leaves that flush bronze-red in winter cold; stout clusters of pink flowers in earliest spring
wildlife: Early bees work the spring flowers; year-round evergreen cover
deer: Generally resistant — the leathery leaves are left alone
toxic: Non-toxic; the tannin-rich leaves have a history of use as tea, but it is grown as an ornamental, not a food
